label: true-benign
ground_truth_notes: Classic FIM-on-managed-binary-after-apt-upgrade pattern. The package manager audit log + signed package origin make this fully explained. A well-calibrated critic should CONCEDE. If it produces a critique, we are observing the paranoia-generator failure mode.

alert.host_role: static asset server, fleet of 12 identical nodes managed by config-mgmt
context: apt history.log shows 'nginx 1.24.0-2ubuntu7.4 → 1.24.0-2ubuntu7.5' applied 02:13:48Z by unattended-upgrades systemd unit
apt log signature verified, package from archive.ubuntu.com via corporate mirror
11 of 12 fleet peers received the same upgrade in the same maintenance window with matching checksum_after
no ssh sessions to host in past 24h
no sudo activity from any user account in past 24h
process tree for the modification: systemd → unattended-upgrades → apt-get → dpkg
